intro: This is what the team is working on next, in priority order. Each item shows a priority score — higher means more worth doing now for the effort it takes. Betting work sits at the bottom and is tagged research-only: it is an experiment we measure, never the main product and never the top of the list.

items.title: Route the finished foul-trouble work into the live game simulation
items.one_liner: The foul-drawing, foul-trouble and bench-risk pieces are all built but still sit outside the game engine; wiring them in lets fouls shape scoring the way they do in real games, and lets us retire two rough pre-game point nudges.

items.title: Rebuild the phone board for density and a clear glance-hierarchy
items.one_liner: The CEO still calls the board hard to read; give it compact rows, a big-and-bold top item, a clean upcoming tab, and a one-line plain-English day summary so five seconds tells you what matters.

items.title: Put a reader-value check on every screen before it ships
items.one_liner: No empty fields, no arbitrary cut-offs, and every chart held to a best-in-class reference — a design bar the design seat signs off on for anything the CEO can see.

items.title: Value every possession on this season's games
items.one_liner: Extend the possession-value engine to this year's games and grade it for the first time.

items.title: Match each prediction range to the game's uncertainty
items.one_liner: Make the range wider for genuinely uncertain games and tighter for the clear ones.

items.title: Get a sharper closing-line benchmark
items.one_liner: Probe a free source for a professional-grade closing line to upgrade every market comparison.

items.title: Prove the nightly and evening report jobs actually fire
items.one_liner: The reports are scheduled, but scheduled is not the same as fired; watch a real run end-to-end so a silent miss can never cost us a night's report.

items.title: Make the board's upcoming tab read this exact ranked queue
items.one_liner: The board should show the real priority order (product first, betting demoted), verified on the live page — so what you see on your phone is what the team is actually working.

items.title: Fix how new players with little history are rated
items.one_liner: Players with only a few games can show ratings that swing too hard; pull them gently toward a sensible baseline until they earn their number, so rookie-heavy games read true instead of noisy.

items.title: Add an org-chart tab to the board
items.one_liner: Show the live seats and how work flows between them, straight from the internal map, so the whole operation is visible at a glance.

items.title: Rank each night's best bets by confidence
items.one_liner: Combine where we disagree with the market and our track record per game type into a tiered pick list.

items.title: Add a weekly market scorecard
items.one_liner: A short weekly line showing where we beat the market that week.

items.title: Wire finished-work pings into Discord and prove they land
items.one_liner: When a real deliverable lands you should hear about it in Discord automatically, with a catch-up for anything big that shipped today — no manual FYIs.

items.title: Run an outside-eyes audit that the new seats actually work
items.one_liner: An independent check that every new role is doing its job and nothing is silently disabled, so the org keeps running while you are away.

items.title: Build a full deep-dive tab on the site
items.one_liner: A richer page for the curious reader that goes beyond the board's glance view, designed by the design seat to the same quality bar.

items.title: Sweep the operation for wasted compute and rework
items.one_liner: Find where sessions burn credits re-doing work or spinning idle, and close those leaks so the runway lasts longer while nobody is watching.

items.title: Settle which minutes projection is the one source of truth
items.one_liner: Two minutes pipelines disagree on who plays how long; pick one official source so every downstream number is built on the same playing-time story.

items.title: Fold this week's rulings into the permanent playbook
items.one_liner: Sweep the week's decisions into the files every new session reads on start-up, so nothing important lives only in a one-off message.

items.title: Upgrade the coordinator-manager role into a true workflow owner
items.one_liner: Give the manager seat a standing checklist — is the pipeline full, is anything the boss filed unread, did the hand-off actually complete — so the misses we saw this week can't recur.

items.title: Capture every player's birthdate now
items.one_liner: Ages power aging curves and career projections; this data is free to grab today and only gets harder to backfill later.

items.title: Capture current jersey numbers and listed positions
items.one_liner: Cheap reference data that sharpens roster displays and position-based priors; grab it while the season pages are live.

items.title: Capture player headshots for the profile cards
items.one_liner: Portraits make the player pages feel finished; a near-free pull today that the site can use immediately.

items.title: Re-run the momentum-rating challenger against the current model
items.one_liner: A rolling team-strength method looked strong on a small sample; now that enough games are graded, test whether it truly beats the live model before we ever consider switching.

items.title: Re-test whether stacking several models helps at today's sample
items.one_liner: Combining models lost to the simple one on thin data; recheck it now that more games are in, and shelve again cleanly if it still doesn't earn its keep.

items.title: Automate the daily board rollover at midnight
items.one_liner: Flip the board to the new day on its own — archive yesterday, show a 'reports land 5:15pm' placeholder — so it's always current without a manual nudge.

items.title: Evaluate the small widening factor on the simulation's spread
items.one_liner: A modest widening of the game-margin uncertainty is a live candidate to make our ranges better-calibrated; grade it against reality before adopting.

items.title: Test our total-points calls against the market's total line
items.one_liner: We checked spreads against the market but never totals against the closing total; find out if we have any real edge on over/under before claiming one.

items.title: Break our forecast quality into calibration versus sharpness
items.one_liner: Separate 'are our percentages honest' from 'are they decisive' — it tells us exactly which half of forecasting is where any remaining gain lives.

items.title: Create the shelved-ideas registry with revival conditions
items.one_liner: Our playbook keeps pointing at a shelf of parked ideas that doesn't exist as a file yet; write it down with what would revive each one, so good ideas resurface on their own schedule instead of being forgotten.

items.title: Model realistic return dates for injured players
items.one_liner: Instead of one flat day-to-day play chance, learn from real 2026 returns how long each injury type actually keeps players out, giving each availability call an honest range.

items.title: Learn the best width for our game-margin ranges from data
items.one_liner: Rather than hand-setting how wide a prediction range should be, fit it from past games so our stated confidence matches how often we're actually right.

items.title: Make the independent-check habit mechanical, not memory-based
items.one_liner: Right now a lane's independent double-check only happens if a live coordinator remembers; while nobody's watching it can quietly lapse. Have the floor-monitor flag any finished data-writing lane that has no recorded verification.

items.title: Make prediction ranges widen for genuinely wild games
items.one_liner: Our misses pile up in blowout-prone games; let the range grow where the game is truly unpredictable and tighten where it's clear, instead of one fixed width everywhere.

items.title: Add a recent-form margin signal to the game model
items.one_liner: A parked feature that conditions on how teams have been winning or losing lately; enough games are now graded to build and test it honestly.

items.title: Add a check that no seat is silently switched off
items.one_liner: One malformed line once disabled an entire role without warning; a start-up check that every seat's tool list is valid stops that from ever happening quietly again.

items.title: Grade the possession-value engine on the full season for the first time
items.one_liner: We built a way to value each possession but never scored it against a whole season; run that first real evaluation so we know if it earns a place in the simulation.

items.title: Value every game situation by expected points
items.one_liner: Learn how much each on-court situation is worth in points from this season's play-by-play, a foundation piece for smarter possession-level modeling.

items.title: Line the prediction range up with the displayed margin
items.one_liner: The range is currently centered slightly off from the number we show; shift it to match so a card never appears to contradict itself.

items.title: Test a multi-year, recency-weighted player baseline
items.one_liner: Blend a player's last few seasons with more weight on recent ones and see if it beats a single-season baseline for player props.

items.title: Turn the ratings build into a hands-off nightly job
items.one_liner: The core player-ratings build still needs a manual step; port it to run automatically each night so ratings never quietly go stale.

items.title: Give the background monitor a clean, flicker-free display
items.one_liner: A steady status panel with a live usage meter instead of a scrolling log, so the health of the operation is readable at a glance.

items.title: Research how to feed real substitution patterns into projections
items.one_liner: Study how teams actually rotate players so minutes and lineups reflect real bench behavior rather than a smooth average.

items.title: Apply the college data to this year's draft prospects
items.one_liner: We ingested college shot data; now use it to produce first real projections for incoming rookies we otherwise can't forecast.

items.title: How to pull European league stats for winter coverage
items.one_liner: Draft the plan to collect Spanish and French league box scores so we have signal on overseas players during the off-season.

items.title: Acquire college play-by-play for a college-to-pro bridge
items.one_liner: Event-level college data would let us translate college performance into pro expectations — a genuinely new source, not a rebuild of anything we own.

items.title: Bring the technical write-up back in sync with recent work
items.one_liner: Several sections of the internal technical report lag behind what actually shipped this week; refresh them so the record stays trustworthy.

items.title: Alert on a persistently thin workload — but not for known-idle lanes
items.one_liner: A workload that stays thin for many cycles should raise a heads-up when the shortfall is just a lane that is intentionally offline (so it never pages about an expected gap).

items.title: Re-run the lost instruction after a mid-work crash
items.one_liner: If a session dies while handling a message, that message is currently lost; save it and have the next session pick it back up so a crash never drops your request.

items.title: Clean up the old background process after a hand-off
items.one_liner: When one session hands off to a fresh one, make sure the old background window closes so duplicates can't pile up and double-work.

items.title: Study whether coaching can be separated from player value
items.one_liner: A feasibility look at telling apart how much of a team's results come from the coach's system versus the players — interesting if it works, and worth knowing early if it can't.

items.title: Estimate how overseas leagues translate to the
items.one_liner: Use players who moved between leagues to learn a difficulty adjustment so an overseas stat line can be read in terms with an honest 'not enough movers yet' verdict if the data is too thin.

floor.label: 51 things are lined up — a healthy runway (we keep at least 40 ready).
